description_long: This object is a concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. Its surface is weathered and stained, with alternating red and white painted stripes that serve as high-visibility markings. The barrier has a trapezoidal cross-section, with a sloped face and a flat top, typical of standard jersey barriers. There are visible signs of wear, including dirt, grime, and some faded writing or markings on the painted areas. The underside and back are less detailed, with the back appearing as a plain, unpainted concrete surface. The overall appearance suggests it has been exposed to outdoor conditions for an extended period.
description: A weathered concrete jersey barrier with red and white stripes, visible markings, and signs of outdoor wear.
description_view_0: The top and sloped front face are visible, showing red and white stripes and some surface grime.
description_view_1: The striped face is seen head-on, highlighting the painted markings and faded writing.
description_view_2: The plain, unpainted back surface is visible, showing minimal detail or markings.
description_view_3: A side profile reveals the trapezoidal shape and the thickness of the barrier.
